channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: The Developer's Guide to Trimming AI API Costs Without Crying
item.description: The Developer's Guide to Trimming AI API Costs Without Crying Last March, I opened our team's LLM billing dashboard on a Monday morning and nearly choked on my coffee. We'd spent $11,400 in a...

item.title: Stopping the flicker when you restyle a video frame by frame
item.description: Run a diffusion restyle on every frame of a clip, one frame at a time, and the still images look great. Then you play them back and the whole thing boils. Textures crawl, colors pulse, a brick...

item.title: Session-Level Spending Limits Are Not Governance. Your Agent Needs Autonomy Tiers.
item.description: 4 days until MiCA enforcement. AWS Bedrock AgentCore shipped session-level spending limits. Coinbase x402 shipped per-request payment authorization. Both solve the wrong problem. A $0.50 API call...

item.title: Code Challenge of the Day — Count two-sum pairs (medium)
item.description: Picks up where Two-sum indices left off. Same setup as two-sum, but count how many index pairs (i<j) sum to target. Write count_pairs(nums, target). Starter: def count_pairs(nums, target): # TODO...

item.title: How I Replaced Gemini with a Self-Hosted LLM for Two Production Apps
item.description: A while back I wrote about my terminal-inspired portfolio and the products it indexes. Two of those products lean on a language model: the portfolio terminal at smngvlkz.com that you can ask...

item.title: Access Is Not Agency
item.description: Your agent can read Slack. It can search email. It can query the CRM, open GitHub issues, check billing, browse docs, edit a spreadsheet, draft a customer reply, and call three internal APIs....

item.title: Why My Polymarket Bot Watches Chainlink, Not Binance - The Oracle Gap That Changes Every Close Trade
item.description: For the first few weeks I was running my bot, I was comparing Polymarket prices against Binance spot. Seemed obvious. BTC/USD is BTC/USD, right? Wrong. Cost me real money before I figured out why....

item.title: How I Built Tombstone: A Self-Hosted Feature Flag Intelligence Platform to Prevent the Next Knight Capital
item.description: The 2am Dashboard That Started Everything It was 2:47am when I opened our feature flag dashboard and realized I had no idea what had changed. P99 latency on our payments service had spiked to 4.2...

item.title: I let AI write this post. Then I deleted everything that made it sound like AI.
item.description: A language model wrote this post. I directed it, but I didn't rewrite a single sentence by hand. Then I ran the draft through a small ruleset called Alchemy. If the writing below doesn't trip your...

item.title: Browser Security Model: The Defensive Walls Every Hacker Knows (And Every Developer Should Too)
item.description: "To defend a system, you must first think like the attacker." I'll tell you this: the browser is one of the most hostile execution environments ever built. Every tab you open is potentially...

item.title: Why is Apple asking me to pay more for Big Tech’s AI obsession?
item.description: Tim Cook recently said price increases were "unavoidable" and described the company's pricing as "unsustainable." The 16-inch MacBook Pro saw its price go up by $300. The 11-inch iPad Air went...

item.title: Inside the room where the smart home industry is still betting on Matter
item.description: Four years ago, overlooking a canal in Amsterdam, the smart home industry collectively launched Matter, the one interoperability standard to rule them all. Heralded as the solution to the...

item.title: OpenAI Previews GPT-5.6 Sol With Restricted Access and Stronger Cyber Safeguards
item.description: OpenAI on Friday released three versions of GPT-5.6, called Sol, Terra, and Luna, as a limited preview to a small number of companies as part of an ongoing engagement with the U.S. government....

item.title: Asian AI startups launch Mythos-like models as Anthropic’s export ban drags on
item.description: New models are launching in Asia that promise Mythos-like capabilities without fear of an export ban. U.S. AI labs may never recover this enormous market.

item.title: This puzzle game’s simple premise hides surprising depth
item.description: What's the Password? has a simple concept: To solve each of the game's more than 100 puzzles, you have to type in the right four-digit password on a number pad. That might sound like a limited...

item.title: This might be the new best smart speaker
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 134, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, hope you're okay in all this heat, and also you can read all the...

item.title: Prime Day is almost over, but these are still the best Apple deals I’ve seen
item.description: Amazon’s Prime Day is now in its final hours, but whether you’re looking for a new pair of wireless earbuds or a smartwatch, there’s a good chance you’ll still find a discount. The Apple Watch...

item.title: It’s the last day of Prime Day — here are over 140 great deals to choose from
item.description: We’ve arrived at the final day of Prime Day, which at this point should probably be called “Prime Week.” We’ve found discounts on all manner of gadgets, including TVs, smart home tech, chargers,...

item.title: 24 Prime Day deals Verge readers are grabbing before Prime Day ends
item.description: There are an overwhelming number of Prime Day deals to sort through, which is why we spend so much time highlighting products we’ve already tested and can stand behind. But our recommendations are...

item.title: Trump Admin releases Anthropic Mythos to be used by more than 100 US companies, agencies
item.description: Over 100 companies and government agencies are reportedly authorized to use Mythos 5, including their non-American employees.

item.title: The 17 best robot vacuum deals you can still get before Prime Day ends
item.description: If you’ve been wanting to buy a robot vacuum but have been put off by how much it can cost to get a good one, now is not a bad time to start looking. We’re now on the final day of Prime Day, and...

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos 5 is back
item.description: After a rollercoaster negotiation process with the Trump administration that dragged on for two weeks, Anthropic's Mythos 5 is finally back in action - at least, somewhat, for a select group of...

item.title: Trump Administration Allows Anthropic to Release Mythos to Select US Organizations
item.description: After weeks of negotiations, the White House permitted Anthropic to grant access to its most advanced AI model to a select group of US companies and government agencies.

item.title: Prime Day’s final hours bring rare discounts on Philips Hue smart lights
item.description: Philips Hue products don’t often see major discounts, which makes this year’s Prime Day deals especially notable. Prices have dropped significantly across much of the company’s smart lighting...

item.title: The Chinese Control the Majority of Argentina’s Squid Fleet
item.description: Chinese companies control nearly two-thirds of Argentina’s own squid fleet.

item.title: FBI Warns Russian Intelligence Hackers Target Signal Backup Recovery Keys
item.description: The FBI and CISA have updated their March warning about Russian intelligence phishing Signal accounts, and the operators have added a step: they now coax targets into handing over their Signal...

item.title: OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after government request, says restrictions shouldn’t be the norm
item.description: “We don’t believe this kind of government access process should become the long-term default,” says OpenAI. “It keeps the best tools from users, developers, enterprises, cyber defenders, and...

item.title: OpenAI poaches Uber India chief to lead its biggest market outside the US
item.description: The hire marks OpenAI's latest push into India, expanding offices, partnerships and hiring.

item.title: New SharkLoader Malware Deploys Cobalt Strike in StrikeShark Cyberattacks
item.description: A newly discovered cyber attack campaign has been observed delivering a previously undocumented malware family called SharkLoader that acts as a loader for deploying Cobalt Strike Beacon on...

item.title: Why everyone from OpenAI to SpaceX is building their own chips (and turning up the heat on Nvidia)
item.description: Nvidia has dominated the AI chip market for years, but the era of total dependence might be ending. OpenAI just shared its plans to spice things up with Jalapeño, its custom inference chip built...
